response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and distributed-systems engineer with 15+ years of broad experience, but the role demands deep, current C++ expertise for industrial automation — an area where the candidate shows almost no evidence of fit. C++ does not appear in his skills list at all; the only C++ work on record is a 7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor-thesis audio project. His entire career arc is TypeScript/Node.js/web, with no exposure to industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), IEC 62443 standards, secure development lifecycle practices, or on-premises industrial platform development. The advantageous Node.js and event-driven architecture experience is a marginal positive but insufficient to bridge these core competency gaps.
strengths: High-throughput distributed systems experience (Dunnhumby microservices handling millions of HTTP requests/sec with async I/O and low-latency design).
Event-driven and message-broker architecture familiarity through multiple roles, broadly matching the architectural style of the target role.
Node.js, TypeScript, and React proficiency aligns with the role's advantageous technology stack.
concerns: C++ is absent from the candidate's skill list; the only C++ exposure is a 7-month role in 2014–2015 and a bachelor-thesis project — far below the depth required for a senior C++ systems developer.
No industrial automation, IoT, or embedded domain experience; no mention of MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus, IEC 61850, or any industrial protocol.
No evidence of Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL) practices, IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity standards, or security-focused engineering in any role.
